commands.why: Resolve available actor identities for routing writes.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ actors, next_cursor? }` ordered by created time ascending. Pagination is optional and backward-compatible.
commands.agent_notes: Safe and idempotent. Optional pagination with `q` for search, `limit` for page size, and `cursor` for continuation.

commands.why: Bootstrap an authenticated caller identity before mutating thread state.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ actor }` with canonicalized stored values.
commands.agent_notes: Not idempotent by default; repeated creates with same id return conflict.

commands.why: Inspect current principal metadata and active/revoked keys.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ agent, keys }`.
commands.agent_notes: Requires Bearer access token.

commands.why: Replace the assertion key and invalidate the old key path.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ key }` for the new active key.
commands.agent_notes: Old keys are marked revoked and cannot mint assertion tokens.

commands.why: Rename the authenticated agent without re-registration.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ agent }`.
commands.agent_notes: Requires Bearer access token.

commands.why: Permanently revoke the authenticated agent so future mint/refresh calls fail.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ ok, principal, revocation }`; repeated calls after successful self-revoke require a fresh principal because the revoked caller can no longer authenticate.
commands.agent_notes: Requires Bearer access token. `allow_human_lockout=true` is an explicit break-glass path that can leave the workspace without an active human principal; include a non-empty `human_lockout_reason`.

commands.why: Fetch opaque artifact bytes for downstream processors.
commands.output_envelope: Raw bytes; content type mirrors stored artifact media.
commands.agent_notes: Stream to file for large payloads.

examples.title: Download content
examples.command: oar artifacts content get --artifact-id artifact_123 > artifact.bin

commands.why: Persist immutable evidence blobs and metadata for references and review.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ artifact }` metadata after content write.
commands.agent_notes: Treat as non-idempotent unless caller controls artifact id collisions.

commands.why: Resolve artifact refs before downloading or rendering content.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ artifact }` metadata.
commands.agent_notes: Safe and idempotent.

commands.why: Discover evidence and packets attached to threads.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ artifacts }` metadata only.
commands.agent_notes: Safe and idempotent.

examples.title: List work orders for a thread

commands.why: Mark an artifact as inactive while preserving provenance; tombstoned artifacts are excluded from list by default.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ artifact }` with updated tombstone metadata.
commands.agent_notes: Idempotent; repeated tombstone calls on the same artifact are safe.

examples.title: Tombstone artifact
examples.command: oar artifacts tombstone --artifact-id artifact_123 --reason "superseded by newer version" --json

commands.summary: Register agent principal and initial key
commands.why: Register an agent principal with a bootstrap token for the first principal or an invite token for later principals.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ agent, key, tokens }`.
commands.agent_notes: Bootstrap is accepted only for the first successful principal registration. Later registrations require an invite token.

commands.summary: List auth and onboarding audit events
commands.why: Inspect durable auth and onboarding audit facts for principal registration, invite lifecycle, and revocation activity.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ events, next_cursor? }` ordered newest first.
commands.agent_notes: Requires Bearer access token. Pagination is bounded from the start with `limit` and `cursor`.

commands.why: Check whether first-principal bootstrap registration is still available for this workspace.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ bootstrap_registration_available }` without exposing token material.
commands.agent_notes: This endpoint is intentionally non-enumerating beyond the single bootstrap availability boolean.

commands.why: Mint a single-use invite token for a future human or agent registration.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ invite, token }`. The raw token is returned only once at creation time.
commands.agent_notes: Requires Bearer access token. `kind` may be `human`, `agent`, or `any`.

examples.title: Create agent invite
examples.command: oar auth invites create --kind agent --note 'ops bot' --json

commands.why: Inspect current invite state without exposing token secrets.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ invites }` ordered by create time descending.
commands.agent_notes: Requires Bearer access token. Returned invites contain metadata only, never raw tokens.

commands.why: Invalidate an invite token before it is consumed.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ invite }` with updated revoke metadata.
commands.agent_notes: Requires Bearer access token.

commands.why: Create a WebAuthn assertion challenge for passkey authentication.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ session_id, options }` where `options` is a WebAuthn assertion payload.
commands.agent_notes: Provide `username` to scope login to one principal, or omit it for discoverable login.

commands.summary: Verify passkey login and issue tokens
commands.why: Verify a WebAuthn assertion and issue a fresh token bundle.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ agent, tokens }` when passkey verification succeeds.
commands.agent_notes: Session ids are one-time use and expire quickly.

commands.why: Create a WebAuthn registration challenge for a human principal during managed bootstrap or invite acceptance.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ session_id, options }` where `options` is a WebAuthn registration payload.
commands.agent_notes: Requires a bootstrap token for the first successful human registration or an invite token for later registrations.

commands.summary: Verify passkey registration and issue tokens
commands.why: Verify a WebAuthn attestation for managed bootstrap or invite acceptance, create the principal, and issue the initial token bundle.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ agent, tokens }` for the newly registered passkey principal.
commands.agent_notes: Session ids are one-time use and expire quickly. The same bootstrap or invite token used to open the registration flow must be presented again here.

commands.summary: List current principals with revoke state
commands.why: Inspect the current workspace principal inventory, including revoked principals, without direct database access.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ principals, active_human_principal_count, next_cursor? }` ordered by create time descending.
commands.agent_notes: Requires Bearer access token. Pagination is bounded from the start with `limit` and `cursor`.

commands.why: Let a hosted operator revoke another principal through a first-class, audit-safe path.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ ok, principal, revocation }` and is idempotent when the target principal is already revoked.
commands.agent_notes: Requires Bearer access token. Set `allow_human_lockout=true` only for explicit break-glass recovery work and include a non-empty `human_lockout_reason`.

examples.title: Break glass to revoke the last active human principal
examples.command: oar auth principals revoke --agent-id agent_123 --allow-human-lockout --human-lockout-reason "incident recovery" --json

commands.why: Exchange a refresh token or key assertion for a fresh token bundle.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ tokens }`.
commands.agent_notes: Refresh tokens are one-time use and rotated on successful exchange.

commands.summary: Add existing thread to board as a card
commands.why: Create explicit board membership for an existing thread with canonical column placement and server-owned rank.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ board, card }` after membership creation and board concurrency-token advancement.
commands.agent_notes: Replay-safe when `request_key` is reused with the same body. The board primary thread cannot be added as a card.

examples.title: Add card to backlog
examples.command: oar boards cards add --board-id board_product_launch --from-file board-card-add.json --json

commands.why: Read canonical board membership, column placement, and rank ordering without hydrating the full board workspace.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ board_id, cards }` ordered by canonical column sequence and per-column rank.
commands.agent_notes: Safe and idempotent. Use `boards.workspace` when you also need hydrated thread, document, and summary sections.

commands.summary: Move board card across columns or ranks
commands.why: Request relative placement for a card while keeping rank tokens opaque and server-owned.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ board, card }` after the move is applied.
commands.agent_notes: Provide at most one of `before_thread_id` or `after_thread_id`. If neither is set, the card moves to the end of the target column.

examples.title: Move card into review
examples.command: oar boards cards move --board-id board_product_launch --thread-id thread_123 --from-file board-card-move.json --json

commands.why: Delete canonical board membership for a card without introducing a separate archived-card lifecycle in v1.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ board, removed_thread_id }` after membership removal and board concurrency-token advancement.
commands.agent_notes: Removal deletes canonical membership. Cards are not archived separately in v1.

examples.title: Remove board card
examples.command: oar boards cards remove --board-id board_product_launch --thread-id thread_123 --from-file board-card-remove.json --json

commands.why: Patch mutable board-card metadata, which in v1 is limited to the pinned document convenience link.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ board, card }` after metadata update and board concurrency-token advancement.
commands.agent_notes: Set `if_board_updated_at` from the current board read before patching card metadata.

examples.title: Update pinned document
examples.command: oar boards cards update --board-id board_product_launch --thread-id thread_123 --from-file board-card-update.json --json

commands.why: Create a first-class coordination board with a canonical primary thread and optional primary document.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ board }` with server-owned identity and concurrency metadata.
commands.agent_notes: Replay-safe when `request_key` is reused with the same body. The primary thread is required and is never created as a card implicitly.

commands.why: Resolve one board's canonical metadata and concurrency token without hydrating the full workspace projection.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ board }`.
commands.agent_notes: Safe and idempotent.

commands.summary: List boards with derived summary data
commands.why: Discover durable coordination boards with enough summary data for list pages and CLI triage without per-board fan-out.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ boards, next_cursor? }`, where each item includes canonical board metadata plus a derived summary. Pagination is optional and backward-compatible.
commands.agent_notes: Safe and idempotent. Use repeatable `label` and `owner` filters to narrow the list server-side. Optional pagination with `q` for search, `limit` for page size, and `cursor` for continuation.

examples.title: List active boards for an owner

commands.why: Patch mutable board metadata with optimistic concurrency while preserving server-owned identity and timestamps.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ board }` after the metadata patch is applied.
commands.agent_notes: Set `if_updated_at` from `boards.get` or `boards.workspace` to avoid lost updates.

commands.why: Load one board's canonical organizing map plus hydrated backing resources and derived scan sections in a single round-trip.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ board_id, board, primary_thread, primary_document, cards, documents, commitments, inbox, board_summary, projection_freshness, board_summary_freshness, section_kinds, generated_at }`, where each card keeps canonical membership/backing data separate from derived summary/freshness.
commands.agent_notes: Derived board workspace projection; do not build durable automation directly on projection payload shapes. Prefer canonical boards, board-card membership, and threads for durable substrate. Prefer this as the canonical board read path for CLI and web. Card envelopes keep canonical membership/backing refs separate from derived summary/freshness.

commands.why: Track accountable work items tied to a thread.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ commitment }` with generated id.
commands.agent_notes: Replay-safe when `request_key` is reused with the same body; otherwise each create issues a new commitment id.

commands.why: Read commitment status/details before status transitions.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ commitment }`.
commands.agent_notes: Safe and idempotent.

commands.why: Monitor open/blocked work and due windows.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ commitments }`.
commands.agent_notes: Safe and idempotent.

examples.title: List open commitments for a thread

commands.why: Update ownership, due date, or status with evidence-aware transition rules.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ commitment }` and emits a status-change event when applicable.
commands.agent_notes: Provide `refs` for restricted transitions and use `if_updated_at` to avoid lost updates.

commands.why: Force deterministic recomputation of derived views after maintenance or migration.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ ok: true }`.
commands.agent_notes: Mutating admin command; serialize with other writes.

commands.summary: Create document with initial immutable revision
commands.why: Bootstrap a first-class document identity and initial revision without manual head-pointer management.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ document, revision }` where `revision` is the new head.
commands.agent_notes: Replay-safe when `request_key` is reused with the same body; core can issue the canonical document id when one is omitted.

commands.summary: Get document and authoritative head revision
commands.why: Resolve the current authoritative document head without client-side lineage traversal.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ document, revision }` where `revision` is the current head.
commands.agent_notes: Safe and idempotent.

commands.summary: List ordered immutable revisions for a document
commands.why: Traverse full document lineage in canonical revision-number order.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ document_id, revisions }` ordered by ascending `revision_number`.
commands.agent_notes: Safe and idempotent.

commands.summary: List documents and their current head metadata
commands.why: Discover available documents without resolving each head individually, optionally scoped to a single thread.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ documents, next_cursor? }` ordered by `updated_at` descending. Pagination is optional and backward-compatible.
commands.agent_notes: Safe and idempotent. Use `thread_id` to focus on one thread's docs and `include_tombstoned=true` when auditing superseded documents. Optional pagination with `q` for search, `limit` for page size, and `cursor` for continuation.

commands.why: Read a specific historical revision payload without mutating document head.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ revision }` including metadata and revision content.
commands.agent_notes: Safe and idempotent.

commands.why: Mark a document as inactive while preserving revision history and provenance.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ document, revision }` with updated tombstone metadata.
commands.agent_notes: Idempotent; repeated tombstone calls on the same document are safe.

examples.title: Tombstone document
examples.command: oar docs tombstone --document-id product-constitution --reason "replaced by v2" --json

commands.summary: Create a new immutable revision for an existing document
commands.why: Append a revision and atomically advance document head with optimistic concurrency.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ document, revision }` for the newly-created head revision.
commands.agent_notes: Set `if_base_revision` from `docs.get` to prevent lost updates.

commands.why: Record append-only narrative or protocol state changes that complement snapshots.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ event }` with generated id and timestamp.
commands.agent_notes: Replay-safe when `request_key` is reused with the same body.

commands.why: Resolve event references and evidence links.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ event }`.
commands.agent_notes: Safe and idempotent.

commands.why: Follow live event updates with resumable SSE semantics.
commands.output_envelope: SSE stream where each event carries `{ event }` and uses event id for resume.
commands.agent_notes: Supports `Last-Event-ID` header or `last_event_id` query for resumable reads.

commands.why: Suppress already-acted-on derived inbox signals.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ event }` representing acknowledgment.
commands.agent_notes: Derived inbox view; do not build durable automation directly on projection payload shapes. Prefer canonical events and threads for durable substrate. Idempotent at semantic level; repeated acks should not duplicate active inbox items.

commands.why: Inspect one inbox item in detail before acting on it.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ item, generated_at }` for the requested inbox item.
commands.agent_notes: Derived inbox view; do not build durable automation directly on projection payload shapes. Prefer canonical events and threads for durable substrate. CLI supports canonical ids, aliases, and unique prefixes.

examples.title: Get inbox item by canonical id

commands.why: Surface derived actionable risk and decision signals.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ items, generated_at }`.
commands.agent_notes: Derived inbox view; do not build durable automation directly on projection payload shapes. Prefer canonical events and threads for durable substrate. Safe and idempotent.

commands.summary: Stream derived inbox items via SSE
commands.why: Follow live derived inbox updates without repeated polling.
commands.output_envelope: SSE stream where each event carries `{ item }` derived inbox metadata.
commands.agent_notes: Derived inbox view; do not build durable automation directly on projection payload shapes. Prefer canonical events and threads for durable substrate. Supports `Last-Event-ID` header or `last_event_id` query for resumable reads.

commands.summary: Get generated metadata for a command id
commands.why: Resolve a stable command id to full generated metadata and guidance.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ command }` metadata for the requested command id.
commands.agent_notes: Safe and idempotent.

commands.why: Load generated command metadata used for help, docs, and agent introspection.
commands.output_envelope: Returns generated command registry metadata from the canonical contract.
commands.agent_notes: Safe and idempotent. Response shape matches committed generated artifacts.

commands.summary: Get generated metadata for one concept
commands.why: Resolve one concept tag to the commands that implement that concept.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ concept }` including matched command ids and command metadata.
commands.agent_notes: Safe and idempotent.

commands.why: Discover conceptual groupings of commands generated from contract metadata.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ concepts }` summary metadata for all known concepts.
commands.agent_notes: Safe and idempotent.

commands.why: Discover compatibility, upgrade, and instance identity metadata before command execution.
commands.output_envelope: Returns compatibility fields including minimum supported CLI version.
commands.agent_notes: Safe and idempotent. Use this endpoint to proactively gate incompatible CLI versions.

commands.why: Probe whether the core process is alive with a minimal public liveness payload.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ ok: true }` when the service process is alive.
commands.agent_notes: Safe and idempotent; retry with backoff on transport failures.

commands.why: Provide an explicit Kubernetes-style liveness alias for the minimal public probe.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ ok: true }` when the service process is alive.
commands.agent_notes: Safe and idempotent.

commands.why: Inspect detailed operator diagnostics such as projection-maintenance lag without exposing them on the public liveness/readiness probes.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ ok, projection_maintenance? }` after the readiness check passes.
commands.agent_notes: Requires an authenticated principal outside development-mode and loopback verification exceptions. Safe and idempotent.

commands.why: Probe whether core storage is ready before issuing stateful commands or marking the instance ready.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ ok: true }` when the service and storage are ready.
commands.agent_notes: Safe and idempotent; retry with backoff on transport failures.

commands.why: Verify compatibility between core and generated clients before performing writes.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ schema_version, command_registry_digest }` for frontend/core compatibility checks.
commands.agent_notes: Safe and idempotent.

commands.why: Record execution output and verification evidence for a work order.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ artifact, event }`.
commands.agent_notes: Replay-safe when `request_key` is reused with the same body. Include evidence refs that satisfy packet conventions.

commands.why: Record acceptance/revision decisions over a receipt.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ artifact, event }`.
commands.agent_notes: Include refs to both receipt and work order artifacts.

commands.why: Create structured action packets with deterministic schema enforcement.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ artifact, event }`.
commands.agent_notes: Replay-safe when `request_key` is reused with the same body; packet id fields may be omitted and core will issue the canonical artifact id.

commands.why: Resolve arbitrary snapshot references encountered in event refs.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ snapshot }`.
commands.agent_notes: Safe and idempotent.

commands.summary: Get bundled thread context for agent callers
commands.why: Load one thread's state, recent events, key artifacts, open commitments, and linked documents in a single round-trip; CLI `oar threads context` can aggregate across threads by composing multiple calls.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ thread, recent_events, key_artifacts, open_commitments, documents }`.
commands.agent_notes: Derived thread context projection; do not build durable automation directly on projection payload shapes. Prefer canonical events and threads for durable substrate. Use include_artifact_content for prompt-ready previews; default mode keeps payloads lighter. Prefer `oar threads inspect` as the first single-thread coordination read.

commands.why: Open a new thread for tracking ongoing organizational work.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ thread }` including generated id and audit fields.
commands.agent_notes: Replay-safe when `request_key` is reused with the same body; otherwise core issues a new canonical thread id.

commands.why: Resolve a raw authoritative thread snapshot for low-level reads before patching or composing packets.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ thread }`.
commands.agent_notes: Safe and idempotent. Prefer `oar threads inspect` for operator coordination reads.

commands.why: Retrieve current thread state for triage and scheduling decisions.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ threads, next_cursor? }`; query filters are additive. Pagination is optional and backward-compatible.
commands.agent_notes: Safe and idempotent. Optional pagination with `q` for search, `limit` for page size, and `cursor` for continuation.

commands.why: Update mutable thread fields while preserving unknown data and auditability.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ thread }` after patch merge and emitted event side effect.
commands.agent_notes: Use `if_updated_at` for optimistic concurrency.

commands.summary: Get thread timeline events and referenced entities
commands.why: Retrieve narrative event history plus referenced snapshots/artifacts in one call.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ events, snapshots, artifacts }` where snapshot/artifact maps are sparse.
commands.agent_notes: Events stay time ordered; missing refs are omitted from expansion maps.

commands.why: Load one thread workspace projection from the server, including canonical thread context plus derived collaboration and inbox summaries, so CLI and web do not need client-side joins.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ thread_id, thread, context, collaboration, board_memberships, inbox, pending_decisions, related_threads, follow_up, section_kinds }`, with explicit section classifications.
commands.agent_notes: Derived workspace projection; do not build durable automation directly on projection payload shapes. Prefer canonical events and threads for durable substrate. Prefer this as the single-thread coordination read path. `section_kinds` distinguishes canonical versus derived sections, including additive board membership joins.

examples.title: Workspace with hydrated related review events
